sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=40, col=33).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 8, level 1.
Valid actions right now: MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - Level: 1 - Board: Black background, gray border. - Objects: - Purple ring: centered around (23, 38). - Purple disk: currently at (40, 33). - White star: currently at (21, 39) (inside/on the ring). - Gray star: currently at (59, 27).
- Action model: Clicking an object on the line shifts it and all objects "behind" it forward along the line's current orientation. - Goal: Move the purple disk to the purple ring.
- Recent findings: - Clicking the purple disk at (53, 22) moved it to the end of the line (59, 27) and shifted the white star forward to (53, 22).
- Plan: 1. Click the purple disk at (40, 33) to move it forward to the position of the purple ring.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 7 of about 22 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The user wants to move the purple disk to the purple ring.
Current state:
- Purple ring: (25, 37)
- Purple disk: (49, 30)
- White star: (40, 33)
- Gray star: (59, 27)
Looking at the image, the objects are on a line.
The line seems to be oriented diagonally.
The order of objects from top-left to bottom-right is:
White star -> Purple disk -> Gray star.
The purple ring is above the line.
Wait, the previous action `MOUSE(row=40, col=33)` was intended to move the purple disk, but (40, 33) is where the white star is now.
Let's re-evaluate the positions.
Purple Disk: (49, 30)
White Star: (40, 33)
Gray Star: (59, 27)
Purple Ring: (25, 37)
If I click the purple disk at (49, 30), it should move forward.
Where is "forward"?
In the previous turn:
Disk was at (40, 33), White star was at (21, 39).
After clicking (40, 33), the Disk moved to (49, 30) and White star moved to (40, 33).
So clicking an object moves it "down-right" and shifts the object in front of it "down-right" as well.
Wait, (21, 39) -> (40, 33) is down-left? No, 21 to 40 is down, 39 to 33 is left.
(40, 33) -> (49, 30) is down-left.
So the line is oriented from top-right to bottom-left.
Wait, (21, 39) is top-right. (40, 33) is mid. (49, 30) is bottom-left.
Wait, (59, 27) is even further bottom-left.
So the order is: White Star (21, 39) -> Purple Disk (40, 33) -> Gray Star (59, 27).
Wait, the previous turn's output said:
Purple Disk: (40, 33)
White Star: (21, 39)
Gray Star: (59, 27)
After clicking (40, 33):
Purple Disk: (49, 30)
White Star: (40, 33)
Gray Star: (59, 27)
So the purple disk moved from (40, 33) to (49, 30).
And the white star moved from (21, 39) to (40, 33).
This means the "forward" direction of the line is (21, 39) -> (40, 33) -> (49, 30) -> (59, 27).
The purple ring is at (25, 37). This is near the start of the line.
To move the purple disk to the ring, I need to move it "backward" or change the line's direction.
How to move backward?
Maybe clicking the object *behind* it moves it forward, and the one in front moves... where?
Actually, if I click the white star at (40, 33), maybe the purple disk moves from (49, 30) to (40, 33) and the white star moves to (49, 30)?
No, usually clicking an object moves it to the end of the line or to the next slot.
Let's test clicking the white star.
Wait, let's look at the image.
The purple ring is at (25, 37).
The white star is at (40, 33).
The purple disk is at (49, 30).
The gray star is at (59, 27).
The line is White Star -> Purple Disk -> Gray Star.
If I click the purple disk at (49, 30), it might move to the end (59, 27) and shift the gray star?
Or if I click the white star at (40, 33), it might move to the end and shift others forward.
Let's try clicking the white star at (40, 33).
Wait, I already clicked (40, 33) in the last turn and the purple disk moved from (40, 33) to (49, 30).
Wait, in the turn before that, the purple disk was at (40, 33) and the white star was at (21, 39).
Clicking (40, 33) moved the disk to (49, 30) and the star to (40, 33).
So clicking an object moves it forward one slot and shifts the object behind it forward one slot.
If the order is Star -> Disk -> Gray Star.
Clicking Disk (40, 33) -> Disk moves to (49, 30), Star moves to (40, 33).
Now the order is Star (40, 33) -> Disk (49, 30) -> Gray Star (59, 27).
If I click the Disk (49, 30), it will move to (59, 27) and the Star will move to (49, 30)? No, only the object clicked and those behind it move.
So clicking Disk (49, 30) -> Disk moves to (59, 27), Star moves to (49, 30).
Then the order is Star (49, 30) -> Disk (59, 27) -> Gray Star (?).
This is moving away from the ring.
I need to move the disk to (25, 37).
Maybe clicking the Gray Star (59, 27) moves it to the front?
Or maybe clicking the ring (25, 37) does something?
I already clicked the ring at (21, 39) and it seems to have shifted the whole line?
Let's check the ring's position again.
Before clicking ring at (21, 39):
Disk: (24, 40), Star: (56, 22) - Wait, this is confusing.
Let's look at the most recent state:
Purple Disk: (49, 30)
White Star: (40, 33)
Gray Star: (59, 27)
Purple Ring: (25, 37)
Line: Star(40, 33) -> Disk(49, 30) -> Gray Star(59, 27).
The ring is at (25, 37), which is "behind" the star.
If I click the white star at (40, 33), it will move to (49, 30) and the purple disk will move to (40, 33) and the gray star will move to (49, 30)?
No, if I click the star (40, 33), it moves to (49, 30), and the disk (49, 30) moves to (59, 27), and the gray star (59, 27) moves to... the front?
Let's try clicking the gray star at (59, 27).
